content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation PPP of the integers {1,2,3,…N}\{1, 2, 3, \ldots N\}{1,2,3,…N}.

Find **any** permutation AAA of {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N} such that, for every index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N),

Ai+PiA_i + P_iAi​+Pi​ is **not** a prime number.

If no such permutation AAA exists, print −1-1−1 instead.

**Note:** A permutation of {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N} is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of the permutation PPP.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ representing the permutation you found.

If no such AAA exists, print −1-1−1 instead.


If there are multiple possible solutions, you may print **any** of them.
constraints: PPP will be a permutation of {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N}
The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 4⋅1054\cdot 10^54⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only choice is A=[1]A = [1]A=[1], which doesn't work because A1+P1=1+1=2A_1 + P_1 = 1 + 1 = 2A1​+P1​=1+1=2 is a prime.
Test case 222: There are two choices: A=[1,2]A = [1, 2]A=[1,2] and A=[2,1]A = [2, 1]A=[2,1]. It can be verified that neither of them work (either 222 or 333 will appear as a sum).
Test case 333: We have P=[2,5,1,3,4]P = [2, 5, 1, 3, 4]P=[2,5,1,3,4]. Let A=[4,1,5,3,2]A = [4, 1, 5, 3, 2]A=[4,1,5,3,2], then it can be seen that Ai+Pi=6A_i + P_i = 6Ai​+Pi​=6 for every iii; and 666 is not a prime.
Test case 444: For P=[6,3,1,5,2,4]P = [6, 3, 1, 5, 2, 4]P=[6,3,1,5,2,4] and A=[3,6,5,1,4,2]A = [3, 6, 5, 1, 4, 2]A=[3,6,5,1,4,2], the Ai+PiA_i + P_iAi​+Pi​ values are [9,9,6,6,6,6][9, 9, 6, 6, 6, 6][9,9,6,6,6,6] — none of which are prime.
